rounds.blurb: The airship Hindenburg burns while landing at Naval Air Station Lakehurst, New Jersey.
rounds.story: The largest aircraft ever built burned in about half a minute while landing; remarkably, 62 of the 97 aboard survived. Filmed, photographed, and narrated live on radio, the disaster ended the airship era overnight.

rounds.blurb: New Orleans under water in the days after Hurricane Katrina.
rounds.credit: Cmdr. Mark Moran / NOAA — public domain
rounds.story: The levees failed in more than 50 places and 80% of New Orleans went underwater for weeks. Katrina killed more than 1,800 people and remains the costliest hurricane in U.S. history.

rounds.blurb: Sally Ride on Challenger's flight deck during STS-7 — America's first woman in space.
rounds.story: Chosen from NASA's first astronaut class open to women, Ride spent the run-up fielding press questions about whether she would cry in orbit. Aboard STS-7 she operated the robot arm she had helped design.

rounds.blurb: Space Shuttle Columbia lifts off on STS-1, the first flight of the Shuttle program.
rounds.story: Columbia's first flight was the first time NASA put crew on a vehicle that had never flown uncrewed — John Young and Bob Crippen rode boosters that had only ever been fired on a test stand. It launched twenty years to the day after Gagarin.

rounds.blurb: Test pilot Mike Melvill celebrates atop SpaceShipOne after the first privately funded human spaceflight.
rounds.story: Melvill rode SpaceShipOne past the edge of space on the first of the two flights that clinched the $10 million Ansari X Prize. It was the first privately built and funded craft to carry a human to space — the proof of concept behind commercial spaceflight.
